description: A designed informational checklist graphic (portrait/square, PNG) from the U.S. Department of Labor on a dark navy-blue gradient background with a faint embossed DOL seal watermark behind the heading. At the top, centered, is the white DOL seal logo above the white text 'U.S. Department of Labor' with the italic subhead 'Self-Audit Initiative:'. Below is a four-item benefits checklist, each line preceded by a checkmark inside a square box, in light blue/white small-caps serif text: 'IDENTIFIES & FIXES POTENTIAL VIOLATIONS', 'REDUCES LIKELIHOOD OF INVESTIGATION', 'DEMONSTRATES GOOD-FAITH COMMITMENT TO THE LAW', and 'ENSURES WORKER SAFETY & SECURITY'. A small white footer call-to-action reads 'VISIT DOL.GOV/SELFAUDIT'. The visual style is clean, corporate, and institutional (navy palette, seal, checkbox list). Messaging technique: straightforward compliance promotion encouraging employers/unions/plan administrators to use the voluntary self-audit program; informational rather than the inflammatory enforcement messaging seen in immigration-agency posts. Target audience: employers, unions, and benefits-plan administrators.
notable_text: U.S. Department of Labor — Self-Audit Initiative: IDENTIFIES & FIXES POTENTIAL VIOLATIONS; REDUCES LIKELIHOOD OF INVESTIGATION; DEMONSTRATES GOOD-FAITH COMMITMENT TO THE LAW; ENSURES WORKER SAFETY & SECURITY; VISIT DOL.GOV/SELFAUDIT
